other_observers: Coen Dexter, Brenda Wright, Nathan Pieplow; first spotted by Coen
specific_location: pond along W.5 road se of Paradox
description: the bird was a fairly large duck, a bit larger than nearby dabblers including Mallard and American Wigeon. The overall shape was quite stocky, due to a short tail, large head, and overall bulky shape. The bill was black, and quite large, with a triangular shape and straight culmen. The bill had a "stuck-on" look to the head.  The cap of the bird was dark brown, contrasting with lighter brown on the rest of the head. There was a small brownish-white spot between the bill and the eye, and another behind the eye. The res of the body was a fairly dark brown, not quite as dark as the cap.Behaviors: the bird was swimming around a small pond, not really in the company of other ducks.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
